title: feat: tag CatGPT bot images
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Publishes successfully delivered bot images with `catgpt` and `catgpt-bot`
- Shares Discord generations with the CatGPT public gallery
- Uploads after reply delivery to avoid retry duplicates

Checks: Biome, esbuild, source/output tagging contract

gist.headline: The cat gallery gets fed
gist.blurb: CatGPT's Discord bot now sends its successfully delivered images onward to the public gallery. The upload waits until after the reply lands, because duplicate cats are still duplicates.
gist.summary: CatGPT bot images successfully delivered in Discord are now published to the CatGPT public gallery with the `catgpt` and `catgpt-bot` tags, with uploads triggered after reply delivery to prevent retry duplicates.
gist.impact: People can discover Discord-created CatGPT images in the public gallery, while bot retries no longer add extra copies.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy CatGPT gallery wall represents Discord generations becoming publicly discoverable: framed pixel cat artworks are carefully added only after a successful delivery stamp. Polly acts as the observant gallery curator, using a tiny curator beret and tagging frames, with a retro terminal and flowering code-plants grounding the scene in the pollinations garden.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush, light blue wings, and a tiny gallery-curator beret, carefully pinning a framed pixel cat artwork onto a warm public gallery wall after a little Discord-style message card receives a checkmark; each frame has small pixel tags reading “catgpt” and “catgpt-bot.” Lime green (#ecf874) glows from a nearby retro terminal beside flowering code-plants and pastel garden vines; cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, nostalgic but beautiful.

image.prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot with pink blush, light blue wings, and a tiny gallery-curator beret, carefully pinning a framed pixel cat artwork onto a warm public gallery wall after a little Discord-style message card receives a checkmark; each frame has small pixel tags reading “catgpt” and “catgpt-bot.” Lime green (#ecf874) glows from a nearby retro terminal beside flowering code-plants and pastel garden vines; cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, nostalgic but beautiful.
